project_type: C++20 Enterprise Cybersecurity Platform with Qt6 QML UI
build_system: CMake 3.22 with ENABLE_QT option. When OFF, Qt is not linked. Non-Qt build must always pass.
test_framework: doctest in tests/ directory, single test executable threatone_tests
verification_instructions: 1. Run cmake -B build -DENABLE_QT=OFF && cmake --build build -j$(nproc). 2. Run cd build && ctest --output-on-failure. Both must pass.
setup_instructions: No setup needed - CMake 3.22 and GCC 11.5 are pre-installed. Qt6 is NOT available.
environment_constraints: INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network. Qt6 is NOT installed so ENABLE_QT=ON cannot be tested. QML files are written as production-quality code but cannot be compiled/tested. C++ ViewModels use #ifdef ENABLE_QT_BUILD guards so they compile without Qt.
contribution_requirements: Non-Qt build must always pass. QML should use Qt Quick 6.8+ conventions. C++ uses ENABLE_QT_BUILD preprocessor guard.
key_patterns: Existing QML uses Qt Quick 2.15 imports (will be updated to Qt 6 style). ThemeManager is a pragma Singleton. Pages are loaded via StackView. NavigationSidebar emits pageSelected signal. AppController initializes subsystems via ServiceLocator. Color scheme: dark=#0a0a1a bg, #1a1a2e surface, #00d4ff primary, #8b5cf6 accent, #4ade80 success, #f59e0b warning, #ef4444 error.
directory_structure: Root: CMakeLists.txt, qml/, src/, tests/, cmake/, docs/, third_party/. src/ has modules: core, database, scanner, edr, firewall, network, ai, threat_intel, etc. Each module has include/<module>/ headers and src/ implementations. qml/ has main.qml, App.qml, components/, pages/, themes/, widgets/.
